summary: Completed comprehensive testing of NSE Autonomous Quant Engine - all backend APIs and frontend functionality working perfectly. Tested 16 backend endpoints with 100% success rate and all frontend features including dashboard, tabs, search, deep dive, watchlist, and portfolio operations.
passed_tests: Backend API: GET /api/ returns root message
Backend API: GET /api/stocks returns 97 stocks from database
Backend API: GET /api/scan/status returns scan state
Backend API: GET /api/stocks/search?q=TCS returns matching stocks
Backend API: GET /api/stocks/regimes returns stocks grouped by regime (SPRINTER: 1, COMPOUNDER: 4, REVERSAL: 1)
Backend API: GET /api/stocks/TCS returns single stock detail
Backend API: POST /api/watchlist adds to watchlist successfully
Backend API: GET /api/watchlist returns watchlist items
Backend API: DELETE /api/watchlist/{ticker} removes from watchlist
Backend API: POST /api/portfolio adds to portfolio with P&L calculations
Backend API: GET /api/portfolio returns portfolio with summary
Backend API: DELETE /api/portfolio/{ticker} removes from portfolio
Backend API: GET /api/market/macro returns macro data (NIFTY50, BANKNIFTY, USDINR, CRUDE)
Backend API: GET /api/market/confidence returns confidence score (15 - RISK-OFF status)
Frontend: Dashboard loads with macro ticker tape, confidence widget (15/100 RISK-OFF), regime columns
Frontend: Stock table displays 97 stocks with proper sorting and formatting
Frontend: All tabs switch correctly (All/Sprinters/Compounders/Reversals/Watchlist/Portfolio)
Frontend: Search input filters stocks by ticker/name/sector (TCS search returned 1 result)
Frontend: Clicking stock row opens DeepDive sheet with price history chart and metrics
Frontend: Add to watchlist from table and deep dive sheet works with success notifications
Frontend: Add to portfolio form in deep dive sheet works with buy price and quantity inputs
Frontend: Portfolio tab shows summary with invested/current/P&L calculations
Frontend: Watchlist and portfolio remove operations work correctly
Frontend: Scan buttons are present and functional (not tested to avoid long scan)
